senses.definition: Describing a plant or fungal structure that has a perithecium (a flask-shaped fruiting body) and forms a drupe-like fruiting body.
examples: The fungus is described as pyrenocarpous due to its perithecial fruiting bodies.
Researchers noted the pyrenocarpous habit in several endophytic fungi.

senses.definition: Plural of pyrenocarp: a fruiting body that is a perithecium and/or a drupe-like structure produced by certain fungi.
examples: Several pyrenocarps were collected for microscopic examination.
The survey documented diverse pyrenocarps on decaying wood.

senses.definition: A genus of fungi in the family Sphaeropsidaceae, consisting of dematiaceous (darkly pigmented) filamentous species often found in soil and plant debris; some species can be plant pathogens or occasionally human pathogens.
examples: Pyrenochaeta species are commonly found in tropical soils.
Infections by Pyrenochaeta unguis-hominis are rare but documented.

senses.definition: Relating to or resembling pyrenoids or the region around pyrenoids; used in biology to describe structures associated with pyrenoids in chloroplasts.
examples: The pyrenodean region showed specific protein localization.
Pyrenodean features were observed in certain algal chloroplasts.

senses.definition: A specific chemical compound (alkaloid) associated with the pyrene family; referenced in chemical or pharmacological literature.
examples: The study isolates pyrenodeine to assess biological activity.
Pyrenodeine was detected in the plant's secondary metabolites.

senses.definition: Describing tissues or organisms that contain pyrenes or pyrenoid-related features; used in biological descriptions.
examples: The pyrenodeous cells were stained to reveal the internal structure.
A pyrenodeous region of the algal thallus was examined.

senses.definition: A protein-rich body inside chloroplasts of algae and hornworts that helps concentrate carbon dioxide for photosynthesis and stores starch.
examples: Pyrenoids play a key role in carbon fixation in algae.
Researchers studied the structure of the pyrenoid in hornworts.

senses.definition: Plural of pyrenoid; multiple protein bodies inside chloroplasts of algae and hornworts involved in carbon fixation and starch formation.
examples: Different species show variation in pyrenoids.
The lecture compared pyrenoids across algal groups.

senses.definition: A lichen association where pyrenoids or pyrenoid-like structures are involved in photosynthesis or pigment storage; used in lichen biology terminology.
examples: The study describes a pyrenolichen with unique thallus morphology.
Pyrenolichen communities were surveyed in the rocky habitat.

senses.definition: An order of fungi in the class Sordariomycetes, characterized by perithecial (flask-shaped) fruiting bodies.
examples: Pyrenomycetales include many perithecial fungi.
Taxonomic keys differentiate families within Pyrenomycetales.

senses.definition: A member of the fungal group Pyrenomycetes; a fungus producing perithecial fruiting bodies.
examples: The survey identified several Pyrenomycete species.
Pyrenomycete fungi are common in decaying wood.

senses.definition: Plural of Pyrenomycete; fungi that form perithecia as fruiting bodies.
examples: Pyrenomycetes are often studied for their ecological roles.
Different genera of Pyrenomycetes inhabit forest litter.

senses.definition: A taxonomic grouping within Pyrenomycetes (often treated as a subdivision or suborder) used in fungal classification.
examples: Taxonomists discuss Pyrenomycetineae in the context of fungal phylogeny.
Pyrenomycetineae includes several perithecium-bearing lineages.

senses.definition: Pertaining to or resembling Pyrenomycetes; describing fungi with perithecial fruiting bodies.
examples: The pyrenomycetous fungi were identified by their flask-shaped fruiting bodies.
A pyrenomycetous habit is common in this group.

senses.definition: A genus of fungi in the family Pyrenomycetaceae; typically forms perithecial fruiting bodies on plant material.
examples: Pyrenopeziza species are studied for their pathology on crops.
The field survey found Pyrenopeziza on fallen leaves.

senses.definition: Stacks of wood or other material set on fire for burning, often used in funeral rites or celebrations.
examples: The bonfire produced tall pyres of burning logs.
On the festival night, people watched the pyres disappear into the sky.

senses.definition: A natural insecticidal chemical derived from chrysanthemum flowers; used in pest control.
examples: The spray contains pyrethrin to kill mosquitoes.
Farmers often use pyrethrin-based products as a biological pesticide.

senses.definition: Alternative spelling of pyrethrin in some contexts; refers to the same insecticidal compound derived from chrysanthemum flowers.
examples: Some labels list pyrethrine as the active ingredient.

senses.definition: Plural of pyrethrin; multiple insecticidal compounds derived from chrysanthemum flowers.
examples: The store sells pyrethrins for garden pests.
Pyrethrins are commonly used in household sprays.

senses.definition: A class of synthetic chemical compounds similar to pyrethrins used as insecticides.
examples: Many household pesticides rely on pyrethroids.
Some mosquitoes have developed resistance to certain pyrethroids.

senses.definition: Plural of pyrethroid; multiple synthetic insecticides related to pyrethrin.
examples: Household sprays contain several pyrethroids.
Pest control programs may rotate pyrethroids to delay resistance.

senses.definition: A natural insecticide derived from chrysanthemum flowers; the plant or its dried flowers used to make pyrethrin.
examples: Pyrethrum has been used for centuries to repel insects.
The cabinet includes pyrethrum-based sprays.

senses.definition: Plural of pyrethrum; multiple sources or batches of pyrethrum or its derivative products.
examples: The supply included several batches of pyrethrums.
Farmers purchased pyrethrums from different suppliers.

senses.definition: Relating to or causing fever; of or relating to pyrexia.
examples: The patient exhibited pyretic symptoms such as high fever.
Pyretic responses were monitored during the infection.

senses.definition: A medical condition characterized by fever or feverish symptoms; used in clinical terminology.
examples: The diagnosis included pyreticosis as a symptom presenting with infection.
Treatment addressed the underlying cause of pyreticosis.
